content:
Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer in Paris. Session date 2026-03-01. Plan: Valencia Marathon on 2026-12-06, target 3:45 (225 minutes → 5:20/km). I want to train four sessions a week only. Counted the calendar: from today to race is ~40 weeks (281 days). That’s plenty of time — I should structure it into a base phase, a specific marathon block, and a taper. Rough thinking out loud:
- Current constraint: four runs/week, plus 2x20 min strength sessions if possible. Three full rest days. Evening runs most weekdays, long run on Saturday. 
- Target peak weekly volume: aim for ~60 km at peak with 4 sessions: long run 30–32 km, two quality sessions (intervals + tempo) and one easy/recovery run ~8–10 km. That gets roughly 60 km if the long run is big.
- Paces: marathon 5:20/km; tempo/threshold work around 5:00–5:10/km; interval/VO2 paces around 3:40–3:50/km for 1k reps; easy runs 5:50–6:20/km. Long runs progressive: start easy, finish last 10–12 km at marathon pace occasionally.
- Phase plan idea given 40 weeks: 12–16 weeks base (Mar–Jun) to raise weekly from current (I’m at ~40 km/week now, long run ~20 km) to a steady 50+ km; 16 weeks specific block (Jul–Oct/Nov) with targeted marathon sessions and long runs; last 3-week taper starting 2026-11-15 into race week (race 2026-12-06). Last long run 30–32 km on weekend of 2026-11-14/15, then reduce.
- Tune-up races: want a 10 km in September as a speed check and a half-marathon in October as a marathon-pace rehearsal and benchmark (target half 1:42–1:44). 
- Injuries/management: keep strength twice weekly 20 minutes, foam-rolling, mobility. If hamstring niggles appear, swap session for cross-training.
Decisions to make now: confirm current weekly mileage and recent race form; confirm exact days for the 4 sessions (I prefer Tue/Thu/ Sat/Sun but can tweak); confirm whether to do the October half and September 10k; confirm peak mileage target of 60 km and last long run date 2026-11-14/15. I’ll commit to a 3-week taper starting 2026-11-15 unless a better plan emerges.
Quick clarifying question: what’s your current weekly mileage and most recent race result (date and time)?

Current weekly average is 40 km (about 25 miles); longest long run recently 20 km. Last A-race was a half on 2025-11-22 — I ran 1:43:30. No full marathon in last 3 years. Work: mostly evenings busy, so runs best Tue/Thu evenings, long Saturday; Sunday can be short recovery run or complete rest depending on fatigue. I can squeeze 20 minutes strength 2 mornings/week (Mon/Fri). Travel/time budget: long runs up to 2.5–3 hours ok on Saturdays. I want two hard sessions per week: Tuesday intervals, Thursday tempo/threshold, Saturday long run, Sunday easy or rest.
Are your four weekly runs fixed to specific days (Tue/Thu/Sat/Sun) or do you want a flexible template that can move sessions around?

I prefer them fixed: Tue = intervals after work, Thu = tempo after work, Sat = long run in morning, Sun = easy recovery 8–10 km (can be rest if long run hard). So final weekly layout: 
- Mon: strength 20 min + full rest from running
- Tue: intervals session 8–12 km total workout
- Wed: full rest
- Thu: tempo/threshold 10–14 km session
- Fri: strength 20 min + rest
- Sat: long run progressive up to 30–32 km at peak
- Sun: easy 8–10 km or rest
With current 40 km/week that will mean gradual increases; target peak 60 km/week is realistic with that layout. I’ll plan the 10k in early Sep and the half in late Oct. Last big long run weekend 2026-11-14/15, then start taper 2026-11-15 for 3 weeks into race day 2026-12-06. Keep strength maintenance through the plan.
Do you want explicit session paces/sets for the Tuesday intervals and Thursday tempos now (e.g., 6×1k @ 3:45/km vs 12–16 km tempo at 5:00/km)?

Yes, concrete sessions please — but on a template level I decide now: 
- Tuesday intervals progression: start with 6×800m @ 3:50–4:00/km with 2–3 min jog recoveries, build to 6–8×1k @ 3:40–3:50/km by peak. Total Tuesday volume 8–12 km including warm-up/cool-down. 
- Thursday tempo: start with 8–10 km threshold at 4:50–5:10/km, progress to 12–16 km with segments at marathon pace (5:20/km) mixed in. 
- Long run: start at 20 km now, add 2 km every 2 weeks to target 30–32 km by mid-November; alternate every 3–4 weeks a cutback week. Every 3rd or 4th long run include last 10–12 km at marathon pace. 
- Easy Sunday: 8–10 km at 5:50–6:20/km or rest. 
Strength: 2×20 min/week focusing on posterior chain and glutes; 10–15 min mobility/rolling after runs. 
I’ll aim to peak weeks at ~60 km, with most weeks in the specific block around 50–58 km to avoid jump too fast. If niggles appear, I’ll back off intervals and keep tempo shorter.
Any specific half-marathon target time for the October tune-up (so we can plan the tempo blocks around that effort)?

Half target 1:42–1:44 in late October 2026. That’s a good check: a 1:42 would imply marathon fitness near 3:34–3:40 but with my constraints I’d be happy if the half sits 1:42–1:44 and the marathon is 3:45. Plan tempo blocks so peak half effort is around 10–14 days after a 2-week taper for the race in October. If half is slower than 1:44 I’ll reassess 3:45 goal — maybe keep target but be ready to adjust to 3:50.
Logistics for Valencia: plan to arrive 2026-12-05, shakeout run and carb-easy day, race 2026-12-06 morning. Hotel near start; no jet lag. That’s the plan.
Any ongoing niggles or injury history I should factor into adjusting volume or sessions?
